sampling_notes.method: 1% orderkey sample (l_orderkey % 100 == 0), full row scan for sampled keys

join_order.description: Full parallel scan of lineitem (l_orderkey + l_quantity). Accumulate SUM(l_quantity) per l_orderkey in partitioned thread-local hash maps. Filter: keep orderkeys where SUM > 300. Result: compact hash set of ~700 qualifying orderkeys.
For each of the ~700 qualifying orderkeys, probe orders_orderkey_hash (pre-built mmap SV hash index) to retrieve row_idx → load o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice. Zero build cost.
For each of the ~700 qualifying orders, probe customer_custkey_hash (pre-built mmap SV hash index) on o_custkey → retrieve c_name, c_custkey. Zero build cost.

For each of the ~700 qualifying orderkeys, probe lineitem_orderkey_hash (pre-built mmap MV hash index) → retrieve all row positions → load l_quantity, accumulate SUM per orderkey. Avg 4 lineitems per order → ~2800 rows touched.

filter_pushdown.note: Applied post-aggregation. Produces ~700 qualifying orderkeys from 15M unique.

data_structures.subquery_agg: partitioned_hash_map(64 partitions, thread-local) for 15M key domain → reduces to compact_hash_set(~700) after HAVING filter
data_structures.qualifying_orderkey_set: compact_hash_set(pre_sized=1024, open_addressing) for O(1) probe in outer query
data_structures.orders_lookup: pre_built_sv_hash_index via mmap — orders_orderkey_hash.bin (SvEntry: key+row_idx, capacity=33554432). Zero build cost. Linear probe: slot = (key * 2654435761ULL >> 32) & mask.
data_structures.customer_lookup: pre_built_sv_hash_index via mmap — customer_custkey_hash.bin (SvEntry: key+row_idx, capacity=4194304). Zero build cost.
data_structures.lineitem_outer_lookup: pre_built_mv_hash_index via mmap — lineitem_orderkey_hash.bin (MvEntry: key+offset+count, positions[59986052]). Zero build cost. ~2800 l_quantity reads for ~700 qualifying orders.
data_structures.outer_aggregation: compact_hash_map(pre_sized=1024) keyed by o_orderkey — ~700 groups, accumulates sum_qty (double). Trivially small.
data_structures.topk_structure: std::partial_sort or fixed_array_sort — only ~700 rows, output top 100

parallelism.strategy: morsel_driven for subquery scan; single_threaded for outer index lookups

subquery_parallelism.description: 64 threads each scan a morsel of lineitem. Each thread maintains a local unordered_map<int32_t,double> for its portion. After scan, parallel merge: divide 15M key space into 64 ranges, each thread merges its range from all local maps. Final single-threaded HAVING filter.

outer_query_parallelism.description: Single-threaded: only ~700 index lookups across 3 pre-built indexes. Parallelism overhead would exceed benefit.

index_usage.purpose: Point lookup of ~700 qualifying orderkeys → row_idx → o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice. Eliminates full orders scan (15M rows).
Point lookup of ~700 o_custkeys → row_idx → c_name, c_custkey. Eliminates full customer scan (1.5M rows).
For each of ~700 qualifying orderkeys, retrieve all lineitem row positions → load l_quantity, accumulate SUM. ~2800 total rows touched instead of scanning 60M.

physical_plan.scan_strategy: single_pass_fused for subquery (l_orderkey + l_quantity co-scanned in one pass); index_driven for outer query
physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for subquery lineitem scan; random_access_with_prefetch for index-driven outer lookups

io_strategy.approach: full_column_mmap for subquery lineitem scan (no zone-map predicate applicable); index_mmap_on_demand for outer query
column_load_order: orders payload columns (o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice) — late materialization for ~700 rows
customer/c_name.bin, c_custkey.bin — late materialization for ~700 rows
io_strategy.prefetch_note: Issue madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) on l_orderkey.bin and l_quantity.bin concurrently before parallel scan begins. Total subquery I/O: ~720MB sequential.

estimated_cost.bottleneck: Hash aggregation of 60M lineitem rows into 15M groups, parallelized across 64 threads with thread-local maps and parallel merge. Warm-cache memory bandwidth: ~720MB / (100 GB/s effective) ≈ 7ms I/O; hash map ops dominate at ~50ms.
